title: Error: mounting volume <XYZ> for container: <ABC>/_data: permission denied
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

While attempting to use rootless podman-in-podman with a pre-existing volume, UID/GID of the host-directory is not set.  I believe this is an intended use-case for the podman container image.  A similar error is reproducible when using the image as root (though it does try to launch the container first).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman volume create -o uid=1000 -o gid=1000 testvol`
   *(options documented in the podman-volume-create man page)*

3. `podman run -it --rm --user=podman -v testvol:/home/podman/.local/share/containers quay.io/podman/stable podman info`

4. `cat /etc/subuid`

5. `ls -lan $(podman volume inspect -f json testvol | jq -r '.[].Mountpoint')`

**Describe the results you received:**

*note: my uid/gid on the host is 4179*

```
Error: mounting volume testvol for container
 1ef...837: mount:
 /home/cevich/.local/.../testvol/_data: permission denied.
```

```
cevich:100000:65536
```

```
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 4179 4179  6 Nov 21 11:48 .
drwx------. 3 4179 4179 19 Nov 21 11:48 ..
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
<podman info output>
```

```
cevich:100000:65536
```

```
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1005179 1005179  6 Nov 21 11:48 .
drwx------. 3    4179    4179 19 Nov 21 11:48 ..
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Nov 11 10:24:13 2022

```

No, but I believe @rhatdan and @lsm5 tried it on a newer version with the same results.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I'm also experiencing this in CentOS Stream9 /w podman 1.3.something.

comments.body: @giuseppe or @mheon do you know who implemented this feature and/or why it might not be working?

This bug is really about the definition of the uid=XYZ gid=ABC options in podman volume on a file based volume.

Should the tool chown the created directory to match XYZ:ABC within the user namespace? Or should it throw an error as unsupported?

I may have found something significant.  If I run my downstream image (based on the podman:stable image) in debug mode, I see this:

```
...cut...normal...stuff...
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 19 0 1000 1 1 1 999 1000 1001 64535`: newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": should have setuid or have filecaps setuid: exit status 1
```

Maybe does `/usr/bin/new{uid,gid}map` need to be setuid but it's not?

They need to be setfcap 
$ getcap /usr/bin/newuidmap 
/usr/bin/newuidmap cap_setuid=ep

> Should the tool chown the created directory to match XYZ:ABC within the user namespace? Or should it throw an error as unsupported?

The case for command-line bind-mount paths, and auto-created anonymous volumes (from the image) seems a lot more ambiguous.  Though maybe they could just take the value of --user (or the one set in the image) and use that for mounts with the `:U` option.  That may be asking a lot though given presence/absence of user-ns CLI options.

For named-volumes though, I think if a user sets the UID/GID options, the expectation is the directory and any copied contents will be chown'ed as indicated.

(IMHO)

While reading the man page more carefully, I realized something significant: My reproducer is specifying arguments incorrectly.  Instead of:

`podman volume create -o uid=1000 -o gid=1000 testvol`

it should be:

`podman volume create -o=o=uid=1000,gid=1000 testvol`
or
`podman volume create --opt=o=uid=1000,gid=1000 testvol`

I'll do some quick tests to see if this is what was causing the ownership issue...

...details, the volume metadata difference does seem significant:

```JSON
[
     {
          "Name": "testvol_wrong",
          ...cut...
          "Options": {
               "gid": "1000",
               "uid": "1000"
          },
          ...cut...
     }
]

[
     {
          "Name": "testvol_right",
          ...cut...
          "Options": {
               "GID": "1000",
               "UID": "1000",
               "o": "uid=1000,gid=1000"
          },
          "UID": 1000,
          "GID": 1000,
          ...cut...
     }
]
```

Checking the actual volume shows the permissions are correct:

```bash 
$ podman unshare ls -lan /home/cevich/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/testvol_right
total 0
drwx------. 3 1000 1000 19 Dec  5 09:55 .
drwx------. 4    0    0 48 Dec  5 10:01 ..
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1000 1000  6 Dec  5 09:55 _data
```
